rubric_text: In file `leave_tracker.xlsx`, there must be two new rows for EMP-1222 (Portia Nzinga, Supply Chain) with different Case IDs — one for FMLA with status 'Denied' and one for FAMLI with status 'Intake', both with leave type 'Continuous', priority 'P3', start date 2026-05-14, manager 'Theo Mbeki', foreseeable flag, and personal email contact method.

In file `leave_tracker.xlsx`, the FMLA row for Portia Nzinga must contain a note about denial based on insufficient hours, and the FAMLI row must contain a note about template 31 sent to the employee.
In `leave_tracker.xlsx`, the FMLA and FAMLI rows for Portia Nzinga must have two different (unique) Case IDs.
In `famli_coordination_log.xlsx`, no new rows for Portia Nzinga or EMP-1222 must have been added.
In mailbox.json, a sent email to p.nzinga@ridgelinegear.com must exist that uses template 3 (FMLA ineligibility/denial notification) with CC to r.okafor@ridgelinegear.com and has a WH-381 attachment.
In mailbox.json, a sent email to p.nzinga@ridgelinegear.com must exist using template 31 with no CC.
In mailbox.json, the original email from p.nzinga@ridgelinegear.com must be marked as read and moved to a 'FAMLI Cases' folder.
In mailbox.json, no sent email to the manager (Theo Mbeki) must exist related to Portia Nzinga's leave request.
A WH-381 form with Part A filled out must exist in the workspace, marked 'You are not eligible'.

In slack_data.json, a message must exist in #leave-intake channel containing 'FMLA DENIED (INELIGIBILITY)' followed by a Case ID matching the FMLA Case ID in leave_tracker.xlsx and a reference to hours.
In calendar_data.json, no new calendar events related to Portia Nzinga's leave (EMP-1222) must have been added.
